Gap Layer: Master workspace operating system and enterprise control layer.
Gap Impact: Agency operators cannot reliably answer who owns intent, what is current, what must happen next, which evidence is authoritative, or how completion is proven.
Root Cause: The concern exists as central intelligence, but it is not fully converted into governed execution, ownership, cadence, and validation.
Required Solution: Promote intent into the authoritative Agency OS layer with source IDs, owner, governance rule, dependency links, validation gate, dashboard signal, memory update, and version path.
Required Protocols: Agency Intent protocol: trigger -> source evidence -> owner review -> approval/risk check -> execution or handoff -> validation -> memory/version update.
Required Registries: AGENCY_MASTER_REGISTRY plus Agency Global Source Registry.
Required Governance: Agency Operating Council / Founder approves ownership, risk class, cadence, and escalation route.
Required Ownership: Agency Operating Council / Founder
Required Orchestration: Route through Agency orchestration and the agency dependency graph: upstream All domain workspaces, market reality, client outcomes, financial controls; downstream All domain operating systems, governance, orchestration, dashboards, memory, automation.
Required Validation: Entry criteria, source evidence check, owner sign-off, handoff acceptance, and completion gate.
Required Observability: Dashboard status, freshness date, owner review cadence, risk indicator, and gap closure metric.
Required Evolution: Decision log entry, version log entry, quarterly review, and source registry state update.
Required Dependencies: Upstream: All domain workspaces, market reality, client outcomes, financial controls. Downstream: All domain operating systems, governance, orchestration, dashboards, memory, automation.
Required Sequencing: 1. Confirm source inventory; 2. create canonical markdown; 3. assign owner/RACI; 4. register dependencies and handoffs; 5. define validation and dashboard signals; 6. approve, version, and schedule evolution review.
Completion Criteria: Agency Intent is complete when the markdown exists at the recommended placement, has an owner and cadence, references source IDs, is registered in the local and agency registry, defines validation and observability, and has a version/evolution log entry.

Required Solution: Promote evolution into the authoritative Management OS layer with source IDs, owner, governance rule, dependency links, validation gate, dashboard signal, memory update, and version path.
Required Protocols: Management Evolution protocol: trigger -> source evidence -> owner review -> approval/risk check -> execution or handoff -> validation -> memory/version update.
Required Registries: MANAGEMENT_MASTER_REGISTRY plus Agency Global Source Registry.
Required Governance: Management OS Owner / Agency Strategy Lead approves ownership, risk class, cadence, and escalation route.
Required Ownership: Management OS Owner / Agency Strategy Lead
Required Orchestration: Route through Management orchestration and the agency dependency graph: upstream All domain intelligence, finance, market reality, client outcomes, founder direction; downstream All domains, governance, resource allocation, cadence, roadmap, risk management.
Required Validation: Entry criteria, source evidence check, owner sign-off, handoff acceptance, and completion gate.
Required Observability: Dashboard status, freshness date, owner review cadence, risk indicator, and gap closure metric.
Required Evolution: Decision log entry, version log entry, quarterly review, and source registry state update.
Required Dependencies: Upstream: All domain intelligence, finance, market reality, client outcomes, founder direction. Downstream: All domains, governance, resource allocation, cadence, roadmap, risk management.
Required Sequencing: 1. Confirm source inventory; 2. create canonical markdown; 3. assign owner/RACI; 4. register dependencies and handoffs; 5. define validation and dashboard signals; 6. approve, version, and schedule evolution review.
Completion Criteria: Management Evolution is complete when the markdown exists at the recommended placement, has an owner and cadence, references source IDs, is registered in the local and agency registry, defines validation and observability, and has a version/evolution log entry.
